div1.entryFree:
Downfall, 1) fall from rank or state, ruin: R2 III, 4, 79. H6B III, 1, 73. H6C V, 6, 65. R3 II, 4, 49 (Ff ruin). III, 7, 217.
2) a going down, setting: from Hyperion's rising in the east until his very d. in the sea, Tit. V, 2, 57. Figuratively: even in the d. of his mellowed years, when nature brought him to the door of death, H6C III, 3, 104 (cf. Rom. III, 5, 128 etc.).
